title: Wren and the Secret Garden Club
synopsis: When third-grader Wren discovers a forgotten garden behind the school, she and her diverse group of friends decide to bring it back to life. But keeping their secret garden club hidden from the strict groundskeeper Mr. Peterson proves trickier than expected, leading to hilarious mishaps and valuable lessons about friendship, teamwork, and standing up for what you believe in.

chapters.title: The Discovery
chapters.content:
Wren Chen pushed her glasses up her nose as she chased her soccer ball behind the school building. The ball had bounced way too hard off the brick wall during recess and rolled into the tall weeds.

"Great," she muttered, pushing through the scratchy bushes. "Now I'm gonna be all itchy."

But when she finally spotted her ball, Wren forgot all about being itchy. Behind a rusty fence covered in vines was the most amazing place she'd ever seen.

It looked like a secret garden from one of her favorite books. There were stone paths covered in moss, old wooden benches, and flower beds that were all wild and overgrown. In the middle stood a little shed with peeling blue paint.

"Whoa," Wren whispered.

She found a gap in the fence just big enough to squeeze through. The garden smelled like earth and old roses. Butterflies danced around purple flowers she didn't recognize.

"Wren! Where are you?" called her friend Maya from somewhere behind the bushes.

Wren quickly grabbed her soccer ball. Should she tell Maya about this place? She'd only been at Roosevelt Elementary for two weeks since her family moved to town. Maya was her first real friend here.

"I'm coming!" Wren called back, taking one last look at the magical garden. She definitely had to come back.

illustrations.description: A young Asian girl with short black hair and round glasses crouches next to a rusty fence, peering through vines at a hidden garden beyond. Golden afternoon sunlight filters through the leaves, illuminating her curious expression. The overgrown garden behind her shows glimpses of stone paths and wildflowers.

chapters.title: Sharing Secrets
chapters.content:
The next day at lunch, Wren could barely sit still. She kept thinking about the secret garden.

"You're being weird," said Maya, unwrapping her sandwich. Maya had curly red hair and freckles everywhere. "Like, weirder than usual."

"Thanks a lot," Wren laughed. She looked around the cafeteria. "Can you keep a secret?"

Maya's green eyes lit up. "Are you kidding? I'm the best at secrets!"

Wren told her about the hidden garden. Maya listened with her mouth hanging open, a piece of lettuce falling out of her sandwich.

"No way," Maya whispered. "You found a real secret garden? Like in the book?"

"Want to see it after school?"

"Duh!"

That afternoon, Wren led Maya through the gap in the fence. Maya gasped so loud that a squirrel ran up the nearest tree.

"This is the coolest thing ever!" Maya spun around, her arms spread wide. "Look at all these flowers! And that little shed! Do you think there's treasure in there?"

Wren grinned. Having someone else love the garden as much as she did made it feel even more special.

"We should clean it up," Maya said suddenly. "Make it beautiful again!"

"Really? You want to help?"

"Are you crazy? Of course I do! This can be our secret project!"

Wren felt warm and happy. She'd found more than just a garden. She'd found a real best friend.

illustrations.description: Two girls stand in an overgrown secret garden, one Asian girl with glasses and one red-haired girl with freckles. Maya has her arms spread wide with excitement while Wren grins beside her. Wildflowers and tangled vines surround them, with a weathered blue shed visible in the background. Warm golden light creates a magical atmosphere.
illustrations.alt: Wren and Maya exploring the secret garden together

chapters.title: The Garden Club Grows
chapters.content:
"We need more help," Maya announced the next morning. She'd made a list during math class. "Look at all the stuff we have to do!"

Wren read Maya's messy handwriting: "Pull weeds, plant flowers, fix the shed, make the paths pretty, find out what's in those scary corners."

"Scary corners?" Wren asked.

"There might be spiders," Maya shuddered. "Big ones."

At lunch, they spotted Carlos sitting alone, drawing in a notebook. Carlos was in their grade but hardly ever talked to anyone.

"Hey Carlos," Wren said, sliding her tray next to his. "Whatcha drawing?"

Carlos looked surprised. He was tall for eight years old, with dark hair and paint stains on his fingers. "Just plants and stuff," he mumbled, starting to close his notebook.

"Wait!" Maya leaned over. "Those are really good! Look at all those different leaves!"

Carlos's face lit up a little. "I like drawing nature things. My abuela taught me about plants."

Wren and Maya exchanged looks. "Do you want to see something really cool?" Wren asked.

That afternoon, they showed Carlos the secret garden. He walked around quietly, touching leaves and looking at everything carefully.

"This is a really old garden," he said finally. "Some of these plants are super rare. And look—" He pointed to a corner near the shed. "Those are herbs! Mint and rosemary and... wow, is that lavender?"

"So you'll help us fix it up?" Maya asked hopefully.

Carlos grinned. "Definitely. But we need to be really careful. This place is special."

Wren felt her heart skip. Their secret garden club was growing!

illustrations.description: Three children stand in the garden as Carlos examines plants closely. The tall Latino boy with dark hair kneels next to some herb plants while Wren and Maya watch eagerly. His notebook lies open nearby showing detailed plant sketches. Late afternoon sunlight casts long shadows across the moss-covered stone paths.
illustrations.alt: Carlos discovering the herbs in the secret garden

chapters.title: One More Friend
chapters.content:
The next week, their secret garden club got another member completely by accident.

Wren, Maya, and Carlos were pulling weeds when they heard someone crying near the fence. They all froze.

"What was that?" Maya whispered.

"Someone's sad," Wren said. She crept toward the sound and peeked through the vines.

Sitting on the ground outside the fence was Zara, a girl from their class. Her hijab was bright purple today, but her face was all red and splotchy from crying.

"Zara?" Wren called softly. "Are you okay?"

Zara looked up, startled. "Oh! I... I was just..." She wiped her nose with her sleeve.

Wren squeezed through the gap in the fence. "What's wrong?"

"Some kids were being mean about my lunch again," Zara sniffled. "They said it smelled weird and made gross faces."

Wren felt angry. "That's stupid. Your lunch always looks delicious."

"Really?"

"Yeah! Remember that rice thing with the yellow sauce? I was totally jealous."

Zara smiled a tiny bit. "That was biryani. My mom makes the best kind."

Maya poked her head through the fence. "Hey, want to see something that'll cheer you up?"

Zara looked confused but curious. "What is it?"

"A secret garden," Carlos called from somewhere in the weeds. "And it's really cool!"

Ten minutes later, Zara was sitting on one of the old stone benches, looking around in amazement.

"This is like a fairy tale," she said. "How did you find this place?"

"Wren found it," Maya said proudly. "And now we're fixing it up!"

"Want to help?" Wren asked. "We could use someone who's good at organizing stuff."

Zara's whole face brightened. "Really? You want me to join?"

"Of course!" all three friends said at once.

And that's how their secret garden club became complete.

illustrations.description: Four children sit together on an old stone bench in the garden. Zara, wearing a bright purple hijab, sits in the middle with a happy smile while Wren, Maya, and Carlos gather around her. Afternoon light filters through overhanging tree branches, creating dappled shadows on their faces. Small wildflowers bloom at their feet.
illustrations.alt: The four friends sitting together in the garden

chapters.title: The Grumpy Groundskeeper
chapters.content:
Everything was going perfectly until they met Mr. Peterson.

The four friends had been working in the garden for two weeks. They'd cleared most of the weeds, fixed one of the benches, and Carlos had even got the shed door to open without creaking.

They were admiring their work when they heard heavy footsteps and a gruff voice.

"What in tarnation is going on back here?"

A tall man with a gray beard pushed through the bushes. He wore dirty overalls and the grumpiest expression Wren had ever seen.

"Uh oh," Maya whispered.

"You kids aren't supposed to be back here!" Mr. Peterson bellowed. "This area is off limits!"

"We're sorry!" Wren squeaked. "We were just—"

"Just nothing! You've been messing with my equipment, haven't you?" He looked at the clean garden paths suspiciously.

"We were making it pretty," Zara said in a small voice.

Mr. Peterson snorted. "Pretty? This is supposed to stay wild! Principal Martinez told me to keep everyone out of here!"

"But why?" Carlos asked. "It's such a beautiful garden!"

"Because kids make messes and break things!" Mr. Peterson waved his arms around. "Look at you! Probably trampled half my plants!"

"We didn't trample anything," Maya said, getting braver. "We were super careful!"

Mr. Peterson glared at her. "Out! All of you! And don't let me catch you back here again!"

The friends sadly gathered their small tools and water bottles. As they squeezed back through the fence, Wren felt like crying.

"That's not fair," she whispered.

"Don't worry," Maya whispered back. "We'll figure something out."

But as they walked back to the school building, Wren wondered if their secret garden club was over before it really began.

illustrations.description: A grumpy older man with a gray beard and dirty overalls stands with his hands on his hips, scowling at four children who look worried and sad. The man towers over them in the garden setting, with cleaned paths and tended plants visible behind him. The children huddle together, looking small and disappointed.
illustrations.alt: Mr. Peterson confronting the children in the garden

chapters.title: Secret Plans
chapters.content:
"We can't just give up," Maya said the next day at lunch. "That garden needs us!"

"But Mr. Peterson said we'd get in trouble," Zara worried. "What if he tells our parents?"

"Maybe we could talk to him nicely," Wren suggested. "Explain that we're helping, not hurting."

Carlos shook his head. "My cousin had him for detention once. He said Mr. Peterson never changes his mind about anything."

They all sat quietly, poking at their food.

"Wait," Maya said suddenly. "What if we could prove we're making the garden better?"

"How?" Wren asked.

"Pictures! Carlos, you could draw what it looked like before, right? And then we take photos of how good it looks now!"

Carlos nodded excitedly. "Yeah! I remember exactly how overgrown everything was!"

"And I could write a report," Zara added, getting excited too. "About all the work we've done and how we're helping the plants grow better!"

"But we still need to get back in there," Wren pointed out.

Maya grinned mischievously. "Leave that to me. I've been watching Mr. Peterson. He takes his lunch break at exactly 12:15 every day and sits in his truck for thirty minutes."

"Maya, that sounds like sneaking," Zara said nervously.

"It's not sneaking if we're doing something good," Maya argued. "We're like... garden heroes!"

Wren wasn't sure about the sneaking part, but she couldn't stand the thought of their beautiful garden going back to being all wild and forgotten.

"Okay," she said. "But we have to be super careful. And if we get caught..."

"We won't get caught," Maya said confidently. "Trust me."

Famous last words, thought Wren.

illustrations.description: Four children sit around a lunch table in a bright cafeteria, leaning in close together as they make plans. Maya gestures excitedly while the others listen intently. Carlos has his sketchbook open, Zara holds a pencil thoughtfully, and Wren looks both excited and worried. Other diverse students can be seen eating lunch in the background.
illustrations.alt: The friends making secret plans in the cafeteria

chapters.title: The Great Sneaking Mission
chapters.content:
Operation Secret Garden began on Thursday.

Maya had timed everything perfectly. At 12:15, Mr. Peterson walked to his truck with his lunch box. At 12:17, the four friends slipped behind the school building.

"Coast is clear," Maya whispered dramatically.

They squeezed through their gap in the fence. Carlos immediately started sketching the Before pictures while Zara took notes on everything they'd already fixed.

"Look how much better the herb corner looks," Wren said, carefully watering the mint plants.

"And I can actually see the path now," added Maya, pulling a few stubborn weeds.

Everything was going great until Carlos said, "Um, guys? What time is it?"

Wren checked her watch. "12:40. Why?"

"Because Mr. Peterson is walking this way. And he looks really mad."

They all turned toward the fence. Sure enough, heavy footsteps were crashing through the bushes.

"Hide!" Maya hissed.

But where could four kids hide in a garden? Wren dove behind the herb bushes. Maya scrambled up the old apple tree. Carlos flattened himself against the shed. Zara squeezed behind the biggest flower bed.

"I KNOW YOU'RE IN THERE!" Mr. Peterson's voice boomed.

Wren's heart hammered so loud she was sure he could hear it. A bee buzzed near her nose and she had to pinch her lips together to keep from sneezing.

Mr. Peterson stomped around the garden for what felt like forever. Finally, his footsteps moved away.

They waited five more minutes before coming out of their hiding spots.

"That was too close," Zara whispered.

"Way too close," agreed Wren.

But Maya was grinning. "Did you see how confused he looked? He knew someone had been here but couldn't figure out who!"

"Maya!" Wren said. "This isn't funny! We almost got caught!"

"But we didn't," Maya pointed out. "And look how much we got done!"

Wren had to admit the garden did look amazing. But she had a feeling their luck wouldn't last much longer.

illustrations.description: Four children hide in various spots around the garden - one behind flowering bushes, one up in an apple tree, one pressed against a blue shed, and one behind a flower bed. The scene shows their worried expressions as they try to stay hidden. Dappled sunlight and shadows create the perfect hiding atmosphere among the lush plants.
illustrations.alt: The friends hiding from Mr. Peterson in the garden

chapters.title: Caught!
chapters.content:
Their luck ran out on Friday.

Everything started normally. Mr. Peterson went to lunch at 12:15. The friends slipped into the garden at 12:17. But this time, Zara had brought her mom's camera to take the After pictures.

"Say cheese!" she called, taking a photo of Maya and Carlos replanting some flowers.

The camera made a loud CLICK and FLASH.

"Zara!" Wren whispered. "Turn off the flash!"

"I don't know how!" Zara whispered back, frantically pushing buttons.

CLICK! FLASH! CLICK! FLASH!

"What in the Sam Hill is going on back there?" came Mr. Peterson's angry voice.

This time, there was no hiding.

Mr. Peterson pushed through the fence just as Zara took another accidental flash photo - right in his surprised face.

"AHA!" he shouted, blinking spots out of his eyes. "I KNEW IT!"

The four friends stood frozen like deer in headlights.

"We can explain," Wren started.

"Oh, you'll explain all right!" Mr. Peterson fumed. "You'll explain to Principal Martinez! Right now!"

The walk to the principal's office felt like the longest walk of Wren's life. Maya kept trying to whisper encouraging things, but Wren felt sick to her stomach.

What if they got suspended? What if their parents had to come to school? What if they never got to see their beautiful garden again?

Principal Martinez looked up in surprise when Mr. Peterson marched them into her office.

"These are the garden vandals I've been telling you about!" he announced.

"Vandals?" Principal Martinez raised her eyebrows. "These four?"

"Don't let their innocent looks fool you!" Mr. Peterson warned. "They've been sneaking around behind the school, messing with everything!"

Wren took a deep breath. It was now or never.

"Principal Martinez," she said in her bravest voice. "We weren't vandalizing anything. We were making it beautiful."

And then she told the whole story.

illustrations.description: Four children stand nervously in a principal's office while a grumpy groundskeeper points at them accusingly. The principal, a kind-looking Latina woman, sits behind her desk looking puzzled rather than angry. Sunlight streams through the office windows, and educational posters line the walls. The children look worried but determined.
illustrations.alt: The friends in Principal Martinez's office with Mr. Peterson

chapters.title: The Truth Comes Out
chapters.content:
Principal Martinez listened to everything. She looked at Carlos's before and after drawings. She studied Zara's notes about all their hard work. She even smiled at the flash photos of their progress.

"This is remarkable," she said finally. "You children have done more in three weeks than... well, than we've done in three years."

Mr. Peterson sputtered. "But they were trespassing! Disobeying rules!"

"You're absolutely right, George," Principal Martinez said. "They should have asked permission first."

Wren's heart sank.

"However," the principal continued, "I think there's been a misunderstanding. That garden isn't supposed to stay wild. It's supposed to be enjoyed."

"What?" Mr. Peterson looked confused.

"That garden was planted by students twenty years ago," Principal Martinez explained. "It was meant to be a learning space, but we lost track of it when the old gardening teacher retired. I've been wondering how to restore it."

She turned to the friends. "It seems like you four have already solved my problem."

"Really?" Maya squeaked.

"Really. George, I'd like you to work with these students. They clearly know what they're doing."

Mr. Peterson's mouth fell open. "Work with them?"

"Think about it," Principal Martinez said gently. "They've shown more care for that garden than anyone in years. Maybe instead of keeping them out, you could teach them even more about plants and gardening."

Wren held her breath. Would Mr. Peterson agree?

The old groundskeeper looked at Carlos's drawings again. Then at Zara's careful notes. Then at the photos of their beautiful work.

"Well," he grumbled. "I suppose they do seem to know the difference between weeds and flowers."

"Is that a yes?" Wren asked hopefully.

Mr. Peterson sighed dramatically. "I guess I could use some help. These old bones aren't what they used to be."

The friends looked at each other and grinned.

Their secret garden club was about to become official!

illustrations.description: The same office scene but now everyone looks happier. Principal Martinez smiles warmly while showing Carlos's drawings and Zara's notes spread across her desk. Mr. Peterson looks grumpy but thoughtful as he examines the photos. The four children stand close together, hope replacing worry on their faces.
illustrations.alt: Principal Martinez reviewing the children's garden documentation

chapters.title: The Best Garden Club Ever
chapters.content:
Three months later, the Roosevelt Elementary Garden Club was the most popular after-school activity.

Wren couldn't believe how much had changed. Mr. Peterson - who everyone now called Mr. P - turned out to be the best garden teacher ever. He knew everything about plants and wasn't nearly as grumpy once you got to know him.

"Wren, can you help me with these tomatoes?" called Aisha, a second grader who'd joined the club last month.

"Sure!" Wren carefully showed Aisha how to tie the tomato plants to their stakes, just like Mr. P had taught her.

The garden was beautiful now. They had vegetable patches, a butterfly area, and a quiet reading corner with the old stone benches. Carlos had painted a new sign for the shed: "Roosevelt Elementary Learning Garden."

"Hey Wren!" Maya ran over, dirt smudged on her cheek. "Mr. P says the pumpkins we planted might be ready for Halloween!"

"Really?" Wren grinned. She loved how excited Maya got about everything garden-related.

Zara appeared from behind the herb section, carrying a basket of mint. "Mrs. Johnson wants to use this in the cafeteria for tea," she announced proudly.

Carlos looked up from the flower bed where he was teaching some kindergarteners about different leaf shapes. "This is the coolest club ever," he said.

Wren looked around at all the kids working happily in their garden. Some were watering plants, others were weeding, and a few were just sitting quietly reading books on the benches.

She thought about that first day when she'd chased her soccer ball behind the school. She'd been looking for a ball but found something much better - a magical place and the best friends a girl could ask for.

"Yeah," she said, pushing her glasses up her nose and smiling at her three best friends. "It really is."

Mr. P walked over, wiping his hands on his overalls. "Not bad for a bunch of kids," he said with a wink. "Not bad at all."

And in the Roosevelt Elementary Learning Garden, surrounded by friends and flowers and the sweet smell of growing things, Wren knew she was exactly where she belonged.

illustrations.description: A thriving school garden filled with children of all ages working happily among vegetable patches, flower beds, and herb gardens. Wren stands in the center with her three best friends, all smiling and dirty from garden work. Mr. Peterson watches approvingly in the background. The late afternoon sun casts a golden glow over the entire scene, with butterflies dancing among the flowers.
illustrations.alt: The flourishing Roosevelt Elementary Learning Garden with all the children

metadata.illustrationStyle: Warm, inviting adventure illustration style. Earthy tones, golden light. Detailed environments with hidden details kids can discover.

questions.question: How did Wren first discover the secret garden?
choices: She was looking for her lost homework
She was chasing her soccer ball
questions.explanation: Wren discovered the garden when her soccer ball bounced too hard off the school wall and rolled into the tall weeds behind the building.

questions.question: What special knowledge did Carlos bring to the garden club?
questions.explanation: Carlos knew a lot about plants because his abuela had taught him. He could identify rare plants and herbs in the garden.

questions.question: Why was Zara crying when the friends first found her?
choices: Kids were being mean about her lunch
She couldn't find her way home
questions.explanation: Zara was upset because some kids were making fun of her lunch, saying it smelled weird and making gross faces.

questions.question: What did Mr. Peterson think the children were doing in the garden?
questions.explanation: Mr. Peterson believed the children were vandals who were messing with his equipment and damaging the area.

questions.question: How did the friends get caught by Mr. Peterson?
choices: Maya fell out of a tree
Zara's camera flash gave them away
questions.explanation: Zara brought her mom's camera to take 'after' photos, but she didn't know how to turn off the flash, which alerted Mr. Peterson to their presence.

questions.question: What did Principal Martinez reveal about the garden's history?
choices: It was built by the groundskeeper
It was planted by students 20 years ago
It was a natural wild area
It was meant to be a parking lot
questions.explanation: Principal Martinez explained that the garden was originally planted by students twenty years ago as a learning space, but was forgotten when the gardening teacher retired.

questions.question: What was the final name of their club?
questions.explanation: By the end of the story, their secret club had become the official 'Roosevelt Elementary Garden Club' and was the most popular after-school activity.

pages.title: Complete the Garden Story
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words from the story!
pages.text: Wren pushed her _ up her nose as she chased her soccer ball behind the school building. When she found a gap in the fence, she discovered a magical _ with stone paths and old wooden _. Her friend Maya was excited to help make it _ again, and soon Carlos joined them because he knew about _. Zara became their fourth member after some kids were mean about her _. When Mr. Peterson caught them, they had to explain to Principal _ that they weren't vandalizing - they were making the garden beautiful!

pages.title: Garden Club Memory Game
pages.instructions: Remember the details! Fill in what each friend contributed to the garden club.
pages.text: Each friend brought something special to the garden club. Wren was the _ who found the garden and brought everyone together. Maya was great at making _ and getting everyone excited about their projects. Carlos shared his knowledge about _ and could draw detailed pictures of leaves and flowers. Zara was good at _ things and took careful notes about their progress. Together, they turned a forgotten space into the Roosevelt Elementary _ Club!
